title: Add non-record submission: Depth Recurrence + TTT + Gradient Checkpointing
body:
Summary                                                                                                                                 
                                                                     
  Non-record submission exploring depth-recurrent transformers for parameter-efficient language modeling:
                                                                                                         
  - **Architecture:** 3 unique transformer blocks × 4 loops = 12 effective layers with U-Net skip connections, per-iteration RMSNorm, and
  low-rank level signals                                                                                                                     
  - **Features:** BigramHash embeddings, SmearGate, test-time training (TTT), Stochastic Weight Averaging (SWA), sliding window evaluation
  - **DDP Solution:** Gradient checkpointing replaces torch.compile for multi-GPU training; torch.compile re-enabled post-training for fast  
  evaluation                                                                                                                                 
            
  ## Results (partial — run interrupted by spend limit)                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                             
  | Run | Steps | train_loss | val_bpb | GPU |
  |-----|-------|-----------|---------|-----|                                                                                                
  | 8xH100 benchmark | 300+ | 2.4452 | — | 8xH100 SXM |              
  | 1xH100 test | 52 | 5.7383 | 3.4016 | 1xH100 SXM |  
                                                                                                                                             
  Training was converging well when RunPod terminated the pod. Applying for compute credits to complete the 3-run statistical significance requirement as well as actually finishing a full EVAL run and getting a full complete score, as Sliding window EVAL couldnt complete before i ran out of the initial 25$ of credits.                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                             
  ## Key findings                                                                                                                            
                                                                     
  1. `torch.compile(fullgraph=True)` + DDP produces NaN with depth-recurrent architectures
  2. Gradient checkpointing is a viable workaround (~2x slower, 18GB peak memory per GPU) 
  3. Sliding window eval (stride=64) should only run at final evaluation, not mid-training
